mechanism_narrative: Persistent near-zero federal funds rate reflects an extremely accommodative monetary policy stance, compressing discount rates and supporting equity valuations, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as long as rates remain at the zero lower bound.
failure_condition: If the average 20-day S&P 500 return during ZIRP periods is not significantly positive (one-sided t-test p > 0.10) or if Rank IC on FEDFUNDS level vs forward return is negative over a 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the unemployment rate of at least 0.2 percentage points signals accelerating labor market recovery, which boosts consumer confidence and spending, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE declines by at least 0.2 percentage points month-over-month
failure_condition: If 20-day forward S&P 500 returns are negative in at least 55% of instances after such UNRATE declines, or average return is below zero.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent positive growth in retail sales (RSAFS) and a declining unemployment rate (UNRATE) indicate robust consumer demand and improving labor market, reinforcing corporate earnings growth prospects and lifting equity prices over a 60-trading-day horizon.
regime_hint: expansionary phase with synchronized consumer improvement
failure_condition: If the composite signal (standardized RSAFS growth minus change in UNRATE) has a Rank IC below 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window, or if the signal's 60-day forward prediction accuracy is below 50% during expansion regimes.

mechanism_narrative: During a post-recession recovery regime defined by quarterly GDP growth exceeding 2% annualized, rising industrial production (INDPRO) signals strengthening supply side, which supports corporate earnings and positive stock market returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the average 20-day forward return during periods with GDP growth > 2% and positive INDPRO month-over-month is not significantly positive, or if Rank IC of INDPRO conditional on GDPC1 regime is below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly release showing housing starts (HOUST) exceeding 700,000 units (seasonally adjusted annual rate) signals a strengthening residential investment sector, leading to positive macroeconomic spillovers and higher S&P 500 prices over the following 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If average 20-day forward return after HOUST crosses above 700k is not positive, or if the success ratio (positive returns) is below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: Negative real federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS minus year-over-year CPI inflation) stimulates investment and consumption by lowering the real cost of borrowing, boosting economic activity and equity prices over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: If the real rate (FEDFUNDS - CPI inflation) has a positive correlation with 20-day forward returns, or if the signal's Rank IC is below 0.03 over any 12-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production (INDPRO) of at least 0.5% signals an imminent economic slowdown, prompting de-risking and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of decline instances, or average return is not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: The 3-month moving average change in nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) provides a persistent signal of underlying labor market health, which is positively correlated with corporate earnings and drives short-term equity price movements over the next 5 trading days.
regime_hint: recovery period with positive payroll growth
failure_condition: If the correlation between 3-month change in PAYEMS and 5-day forward S&P 500 returns is negative over any 1-year period, or Rank IC < 0.
